info.title: CIA Internal API
info.description: Internal REST API for the Core Insurance Application — staff, System Auditors, and system-to-system use. Authentication via Keycloak JWT (Bearer token).

bearerJwt.description: Keycloak RS256 JWT. Obtain via Keycloak login at your tenant realm.

idDocumentUrl.description: MinIO/S3 path to uploaded ID document

idExpiryDate.description: Required for Driver's Licence and Passport; must not be expired at onboarding

cacCertificateUrl.description: MinIO/S3 path to uploaded CAC certificate

type.description: SYSTEM reports are immutable; clone into CUSTOM to modify

config.description: ReportConfig JSONB: {fields, filters, groupBy, sortBy, sortDir, chart}

tags.name: Audit Logs
tags.description: Search and view the full system audit trail across all modules

tags.name: Login & Session Logs
tags.description: Login, logout, and failed authentication event recording and retrieval

tags.name: Audit Alerts
tags.description: Real-time anomaly alerts — failed logins, bulk deletes, off-hours activity, large approvals

tags.name: Audit Configuration
tags.description: Alert thresholds and data retention settings (System Admin only for writes)

get.description: Paginated, filterable audit trail. Filter by entityType, entityId, userId, action, or date range.

403.description: Insufficient role — requires AUDIT_VIEW or SETUP_UPDATE

get.description: Filterable by userId or date range. Requires AUDIT_VIEW or SETUP_UPDATE role.

post.description: Called by the frontend after successful Keycloak authentication. Requires a valid JWT.
Called by the frontend on logout. Requires a valid JWT.
**Public endpoint — no JWT required.** Called when JWT validation fails for a known user. Triggers failed-login alert detection.

get.description: Returns all alerts or unacknowledged-only when `unacknowledgedOnly=true`. Ordered newest first.

post.description: Marks the alert as acknowledged. The acknowledging user is recorded.

get.description: Returns the current alert thresholds and retention policy for this tenant. Requires AUDIT_VIEW or SETUP_UPDATE.

put.description: Updates alert thresholds and retention period. **Requires SETUP_UPDATE role (System Admin only).**

get.description: Streams a CSV file containing audit log entries matching the supplied filters. All filters are optional.
All audit events performed by a specific user in a date range.
All audit events for a specific entity type (module) in a date range.
All APPROVE and REJECT events across every module in a date range.
Full before/after change history for a specific entity. Returns old_value and new_value JSONB snapshots.

200.description: Paginated change events with before/after snapshots

get.description: Login, logout, and failed authentication events in a date range.
Ranked list of all users by total action count in a date range.

Returns 8 aggregated KPIs for the back-office home screen: active policies, open claims, pending approvals, premiums MTD, claims reserve total, renewals due in 30 days, outstanding premium, and RI utilisation %.

get.summary: Pending approval counts by entity type
get.description: Returns the count of items in PENDING_APPROVAL status for each entity type across all modules.
Returns monthly premium written and claims incurred for the last 6 months, plus a computed loss ratio percentage per month. Used for the dashboard sparkline chart.

get.summary: Policies expiring in the next 7 days
get.description: Returns one entry per day for the next 7 days, each with the count of ACTIVE policies whose end date falls on that day. Always returns exactly 7 entries; days with no renewals have count = 0.

get.summary: Last 10 audit log entries across all modules
get.description: Returns the 10 most recent audit log entries from the system-wide audit trail, ordered by timestamp descending. Used for the Recent Activity feed on the dashboard.

get.summary: Global search across policies, claims, customers, and quotes
get.description: Case-insensitive ILIKE search across policies (by policy_number), claims (by claim_number), customers (by name), and quotes (by quote_number). Returns up to 5 results per entity type, capped at 20 total. Minimum query length is 2 characters.
Returns a paginated list of all customers. Filterable by type (INDIVIDUAL/CORPORATE) and KYC status.

get.summary: Search customers by name, email, or phone

post.description: Creates an individual customer record and triggers KYC verification. Accepts `multipart/form-data`. The `idDocument` file must be JPG or PNG, max 5 MB. `idExpiryDate` is **required and must not be expired** when `idType` is `DRIVERS_LICENSE` or `PASSPORT`.

idExpiryDate.description: Required for DRIVERS_LICENSE and PASSPORT. Must be ≥ today.

400.description: Validation error — e.g. expired ID document

post.description: Creates a corporate customer record and triggers KYC (RC number verification + director ID checks). Accepts `multipart/form-data`. `cacCertificate` must be JPG or PNG, max 5 MB. `cacIssuedDate` is required. Each director's `idDocument` (indexed as `directorIdDocuments`) must be provided. Director `idExpiryDate[i]` is required and must not be expired for DRIVERS_LICENSE/PASSPORT.

directors[0].fullName.description: Repeat for each director with indexed fields

directors[0].idExpiryDate.description: Required for DRIVERS_LICENSE/PASSPORT. Must be ≥ today.

cacCertificate.description: CAC certificate — JPG or PNG, max 5 MB

directorIdDocuments.description: One file per director in the same order as directors[]. JPG or PNG, max 5 MB each

400.description: Validation error — e.g. expired director ID

post.description: Re-runs KYC verification for a customer whose KYC status is FAILED or PENDING. Returns 422 if KYC has already passed.

get.description: Returns all active report definitions the caller can view. Filter by category to narrow results. Reports the caller cannot view are invisible (not returned with an access-denied flag).

put.description: Only CUSTOM reports can be updated. SYSTEM reports are immutable — clone them first.

delete.description: Only CUSTOM reports can be deleted. SYSTEM reports cannot be deleted.

post.summary: Clone a SYSTEM report into a CUSTOM report
post.description: Creates a new CUSTOM report pre-populated with the source report's config. The user can then edit and save it.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name for the cloned report (defaults to source name + ' (Copy)')

post.summary: Run a report — returns JSON result

filters.description: Key-value map of filter values matching the report's config.filters

200.description: Report result with columns, rows, and totalRows

post.summary: Run a report — streams CSV download
Run a report — returns PDF download

get.summary: List pinned reports for the current user

post.summary: Pin a report to the home page
post.description: Pins are per-user. Regulatory reports (REGULATORY category) cannot be pinned.

get.summary: List report access policies for an access group
get.description: System Admin only (`reports:manage_access`). Returns all category-level and report-level access policies for the specified group.

put.summary: Upsert a category-level or report-level access policy
put.description: Resolution order: report-level policy wins over category-level. A user with no matching policy cannot see the report at all.
